rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT / Amended Notice Of Hearing On Plaintiffs Motion For Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Of Claims
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 13, 2025 line 2. PLAINTIFF DAVID STANTON AN INDIVIDUAL MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT / AMENDED NOTICE OF HEARING ON PLAINTIFFS MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF CLAIMS is DENIED. There are triable issues of fact regarding the amount of money owed and Defendant's affirmative defenses. Defendant claims that Plaintiff breached the contract, prevented Defendant's performance, and has unclean hands. With all reasonable inferences made in Defendant's favor, Defendant's evidence raises questions of fact regarding whether the breaches were material, whether performance was impossible, and whether Plaintiff committed the acts that constitute unclean hands. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: ABDALLAH ELHANIA ET AL VS. AIRBNB, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Disqualify Defense Law Firms
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 13, 2025 line 3. PLAINTIFF ABDALLAH ELHANIA, ZAHRA ELMENJAOUI NOTICE OF MOTION TO DISQUALIFY DEFENSE LAW FIRMS is DENIED without prejudice. Plaintiff's motion is a discovery motion masked as a motion to disqualify. Underlying Plaintiff's grounds to disqualify counsel are allegations that Defense counsel issued subpoenas without serving Plaintiff either at all or improperly, in violation of CCP sections 2025.240(a) and 1987.5. Plaintiff further claims that the records received as a result of those subpoenas are confidential under Welfare & Institutions Code section 827(a) and cannot be obtained via a subpoena. Defendants deny any claims they violated their discovery obligations. Department 501 is not in a position to rule on the motion to disqualify based on alleged violations of the Discovery Act unless Plaintiff first obtains rulings adjudicating the underlying discovery disputes in the proper department. Plaintiff should file a motion under the Civil Discovery Act with Department 302 in the first instance. Based on that outcome and if appropriate, Plaintiff is welcome to re-file their motion to disqualify in Dept. 501 at that time. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tent ative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: BWF FORGE TL PROPERTIES OWNER, LLC VS. JASON SKELTON ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 13, 2025 line 13. DEFENDANT JASON SKELTON MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS is GRANTED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. No opposition filed. The Complaint fails to state a cause of action. (CCP 438(c)(1)(B)(ii).) The Notice to Pay Rent or Quit is fatally defective because the Form 1007 Rent Board attachment is not the version that was in effect at the time the Notice was served. The outdated Form 1007 contains materially different information than the current version and is not the mandatory attachment referred to by Section 37.9(c) of the Rent Ordinance as of the time of service. This defect cannot be cured by amending the pleadings. Judgment shall enter for defendant and against plaintiff. =(501/VMH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tent ative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
